oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Evaluation of welfare in bulls subjected to weekly electroejaculation
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Schwartz, D.W.
Armstrong, C.
Gassipard, K.
Cowley, J.
Foradori, C.
Bayne, J.
Chaves-Cordoba, B.
Klabnik, J.
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Electroejaculation (EEJ) is a consistent and reliable method for semen collection but is subject to welfare concerns due to oc­casional outward vocalization and perceived discomfort. There is no study which has investigated the impacts of repeated EEJ. The first objective was to compare plasma Substance P concen­trations, pen score (PS), chute score (CS), and exit velocity (EV) between control bulls (CTL) and treatment (TX) that experi­enced electroejaculation weekly. The second objective was to compare the same parameters for the last 2 weeks of the weekly electroejaculations and 2 alternating days in which both groups were processed but TX bulls were not ejaculated. For both ob­jectives, we hypothesized that there would not be significant differences in Substance P, PS, CS or EV between groups (for Objective 1 and 2) or within group (for Objective 2).
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 240
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
